verses.text: Saul now openly orders Jonathan and his servants to kill David. But Jonathan, loving David, warns him and resolves to plead his cause. When evil gives a command, love finds a way to protect the innocent. Loyalty to a friend can outweigh loyalty to a father's sin.

Saul now openly orders Jonathan and his servants to kill David. But Jonathan, loving David, warns him and resolves to plead his cause. When evil gives a command, love finds a way to protect the innocent. Loyalty to a friend can outweigh loyalty to a father's sin.

Saul now openly orders Jonathan and his servants to kill David. But Jonathan, loving David, warns him and resolves to plead his cause. When evil gives a command, love finds a way to protect the innocent. Loyalty to a friend can outweigh loyalty to a father's sin.

Jonathan reminds Saul how David risked his life for Israel and 'sinned not against thee.' Saul listens and swears David shall not die. A brave, honest word can turn back a murderous heart, at least for a time. Jonathan's courage wins David a season of peace.

But after a fresh victory the old spirit returns, and Saul again hurls his javelin at David, who slips away into the night. Jealousy soothed is not jealousy cured; the truce was thin. A heart that will not deal with its envy keeps relapsing into rage.

Saul posts watchers to kill David at dawn, but Michal lowers him through a window and lays an image in the bed to buy time. God uses even a wife's quick wit to preserve His anointed. The man God means to keep, no night ambush can take.

The trick is found out, and Michal protects herself with a false excuse. Her courage in saving David is mingled with a readiness to lie. Often our faith and our fear, our boldness and our weakness, are tangled together — and still God works His purpose through us.

David flees to Samuel at Ramah, and when Saul's men come to seize him, the Spirit of God falls on them and they prophesy instead. God can disarm pursuers without a blow, turning hunters into worshippers. No weapon reaches the servant the LORD has fenced about.

Saul comes himself, and the Spirit overwhelms even him; he strips and prophesies all day, and men marvel, 'Is Saul also among the prophets?' God can override a man's purpose for a moment, yet such an experience is not conversion. Saul prophesies, but his heart is unchanged.
